Title: The FUSED LEAVES1-ADHERENT1 regulatory module is required for maize cuticle development and organ separation

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: KCS family; FAE1/type III polyketide synthase-like; ACP_syn_III_C domain
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: cuticular wax biosynthesis; very-long-chain fatty acid elongation
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: VLCFA elongation supplying cuticular wax components
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: upstream/committed elongation step in wax precursor formation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: 3-keto very-long-chain acyl-CoA elongated intermediates leading to VLCFAs
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: catalyzes condensation in the fatty acid elongase cycle to extend acyl-CoA chains for production of very-long-chain wax precursors
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: aerial epidermal surface of seedling leaves, coleoptiles, tassels, glumes, anthers
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: prevents organ fusion, limits nonstomatal water loss, reduces chlorophyll leaching, contributes to drought protection and epidermal barrier function
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of AD1 reduces cuticular wax accumulation and epicuticular wax crystals, increases cuticle permeability and water loss, and causes organ fusion defects.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AD1 was positional-cloned as GRMZM2G167438/Zm00001d032728 and encodes a KCS enzyme. 2. Independent ad1 alleles carried truncating mutations disrupting conserved catalytic domains. 3. ad1 mutants showed reduced total wax load and broad decreases in VLC wax classes, especially very-long-chain alcohols, aldehydes, fatty acids and wax esters. 4. AD1 is epidermally expressed and ER-localized, consistent with a role in wax biosynthesis required for organ separation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: positional cloning; allele sequencing; mutant phenotyping; SEM of wax crystals; GC-MS/GC-FID wax profiling; qRT-PCR; in situ hybridization; transient subcellular localization
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: highest in tassels, ears and seedling leaves; strongest in epidermal cells of germinating shoots, embryos and immature tassels
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: highest in tassels, ears and seedling leaves; epidermal layer enriched
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: drought treatment; dark acclimation for water-loss assay; ethanol chlorophyll leaching assay; toluidine blue permeability assay
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: paper and discussion state KCS enzymes catalyze the rate-limiting step in VLCFA production
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: bulked segregant whole-genome sequencing; wax metabolite profiling by GC-MS/GC-FID
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AD1 corresponds to ZmKCS19 and belongs to the conserved but previously uncharacterized h subclass of KCS genes. Chemical data support a role in formation of very-long-chain wax components, but chain-length specificity was not clearly resolved.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: loss-of-function causes severe epidermal fusion and drought sensitivity
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful target for improving cuticle integrity, organ separation, and drought-associated barrier traits, but reduced function has severe developmental penalties

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: cuticular wax and cutin biosynthesis, transport, cuticle development, stress-associated lipid barrier regulation
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: direct transcriptional activation and context-dependent repression
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: AD1; GLOSSY26/ZmCER10/ZmECR; ZmBODYGUARD1 (ZmBDG1); GLOSSY8A/ZmKCR; GLOSSY2/ZmCER2; several KCS genes; ZmABCG11 family; GLOSSY6; LTP genes including ZmLTP1.6 and ZmLTP1.2
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: directly binds CCAACC-containing regulatory regions; activates AD1 and multiple cuticle-related genes; some bound genes were also upregulated in mutants, indicating context-dependent repression for a subset
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: developmental program during embryogenesis/seedling development; likely drought, ABA, pathogen and light-related inputs inferred from bound and responsive genes
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: possible network-level compensatory regulation via other MYBs; no direct feedback loop conclusively established
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: drought stress; ABA-related signaling context; light-related pathways; early seedling/embryo development
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: aerial epidermal surface of coleoptiles, leaves and tassel organs
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: epidermal barrier formation, prevention of organ fusion, limitation of water loss, drought protection
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of FDL1 reduces cuticle integrity and wax deposition, causes seedling leaf fusion, enhances ad1 defects, and positively regulates AD1 expression.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: key positive regulator of maize cuticle formation during early development; directly controls AD1 and many additional cuticle biosynthesis/transport genes
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FDL1 encodes an epidermally expressed R2R3-MYB TF with seedling cuticle and organ-fusion phenotypes. 2. DAP-seq identified 10,028 binding peaks and 2,533 nearby target genes, enriched for the CCAACCAC motif. 3. FDL1 directly binds the AD1 5' region and activates AD1 transcription through CCAACC motifs, confirmed by EMSA and dual-LUC assays. 4. fdl1 mutants showed large transcriptional reprogramming, and ad1;fdl1 double mutants had enhanced permeability and drought-related defects, indicating synergistic function in cuticle development.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant phenotyping; double-mutant analysis; in situ hybridization; DAP-seq; motif analysis; EMSA; qRT-PCR; transient dual-luciferase transactivation assay; RNA-seq
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: strong in epidermal layer of young leaves and tassels; active during embryogenesis and early seedling development
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: epidermal layer of young leaves and tassels; early coleoptiles/embryos
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: RNA-seq of fdl1 mutants showed downregulation of genes enriched in epigenetic regulation GO terms, but no direct epigenetic mechanism for FDL1 itself was reported
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: FDL1 is the maize ortholog of MYB94-type regulators. It can behave as activator or repressor depending on target/context. No tassel defects were observed in fdl1-Mu single mutants, unlike ad1.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: loss-of-function increases water loss, cuticle permeability, drought sensitivity, and leaf fusion
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: promising regulator for engineering cuticle integrity and water-use efficiency, but manipulation may broadly reprogram stress and developmental pathways
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter used for effector construct in transactivation assay
